title: Purandar Fort
tagline: Purandar Fort — heritage city of Maharashtra

overview.short: Purandar Fort is a mountain fort in Pune district in the Western Indian state of Maharashtra, India.
overview.description: Purandar Fort is a mountain fort in Pune district in the Western Indian state of Maharashtra, India. The fort stands at 1,374 metres (4,508 ft) above sea level in the Western Ghats, 50 kilometres (31 mi) to the southeast of Pune. The twin forts of Purandar (or Indraneel Parwat) and Vajragad (or Rudramal) of which the latter is the smaller of the two, is located on the eastern side of the main fort rising 1,347 metres (4,419 ft) above sea level. The village of Purandar takes its name from this fort.

topPlaces.name: Balaji Temple, Ketkawla
topPlaces.description: Balaji Temple of Ketkawle, is copy of the original Tirumala Venkateswara Temple located 60 km from Pune, Maharashtra. It is on Pune-Bangalore highway.

items.note: Balaji Temple of Ketkawle, is copy of the original Tirumala Venkateswara Temple located 60 km from Pune, Maharashtra. It is on Pune-Bangalore highway.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Purandar Fort?
faq.a: The best time to visit Purandar Fort is Apr – Jun, Sep – Oct. Summers are 10–22°C and winters are -6–10°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Purandar Fort?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Pune Airport (34 km) and the nearest railway station is Pune Junction (30 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Purandar Fort?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Purandar Fort at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Purandar Fort?
faq.a: Stays in Purandar Fort start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Purandar Fort?
faq.a: The top attractions are Balaji Temple, Ketkawla.
